     The Numbers Business Report

The Martian (2015)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $228,433,663Details
International Box Office $425,175,444
Worldwide Box Office $653,609,107
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $20,004,992
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $30,785,278
Total $50,790,270
Further financial details...

Synopsis

During a manned mission to Mars, Astronaut Mark Watney is presumed dead after a fierce storm and left behind by his crew. But Watney has survived and finds himself stranded and alone on the hostile planet. With only meager supplies, he must draw upon his ingenuity, wit and spirit to subsist and find a way to signal to Earth that he is alive. Millions of miles away, NASA and a team of international scientists work tirelessly to bring “the Martian” home, while his crewmates concurrently plot a daring, if not impossible, rescue mission. As these stories of incredible bravery unfold, the world comes together to root for Watney’s safe return.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$54,308,575 (23.8% of total gross)
Legs:4.21 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:34.9% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$108,000,000 (worldwide box office is 6.1 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,831 opening theaters/3,854 max. theaters, 8.3 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $323,977,133

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: October 2nd, 2015 (Wide) by 20th Century Fox
International Releases: September 30th, 2015 (Wide) (Philippines)
September 30th, 2015 (Wide) (Indonesia)
October 1st, 2015 (Wide) (Hong Kong)
October 1st, 2015 (Wide) (Hungary)
October 1st, 2015 (Wide) (Chile)
Video Release: December 22nd, 2015 by Fox Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for some strong language, injury images, and brief nudity.
(Rating bulletin 2389, 9/2/2015)
Running Time: 130 minutes
Award nomination: 2016 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Award win: Golden Globe Winner Best Picture—Musical or Comedy
Cast, crew, or production detail: Breaking the Fourth Wall, Epilogue, Intertitle, Scene in End Credits, Voiceover/Narration
Film format: 3-D, IMAX: DMR
Geographical setting: Set on Mars
Plot point: Astronaut, Disaster, Marooned, NASA, Rescue, Extreme Weather
Social setting: Space Program
Subgenre: Survival Adventure
Time period setting: 2030s
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Science Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Scott Free Films, Kinberg Genre
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Full financial estimates for this film, including domestic and international box office, video sales, video rentals, TV and ancillary revenue are available through our research services. For more information, please contact us at research@the-numbers.com.


  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

During a manned mission to Mars, Astronaut Mark Watney is presumed dead after a fierce storm and left behind by his crew. But Watney has survived and finds himself stranded and alone on the hostile planet. With only meager supplies, he must draw upon his ingenuity, wit and spirit to subsist and find a way to signal to Earth that he is alive. Millions of miles away, NASA and a team of international scientists work tirelessly to bring “the Martian” home, while his crewmates concurrently plot a daring, if not impossible, rescue mission. As these stories of incredible bravery unfold, the world comes together to root for Watney’s safe return.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$54,308,575 (23.8% of total gross)

Domestic Share:34.9% (domestic box office/worldwide)

Theater counts:3,831 opening theaters/3,854 max. theaters, 8.3 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $323,977,133

Movie Details

Running Time: 130 minutes
Award nomination: 2016 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Award win: Golden Globe Winner Best Picture—Musical or Comedy
Cast, crew, or production detail: Breaking the Fourth Wall, Epilogue, Intertitle, Scene in End Credits, Voiceover/Narration
Film format: 3-D, IMAX: DMR
Geographical setting: Set on Mars
Plot point: Astronaut, Disaster, Marooned, NASA, Rescue, Extreme Weather
Social setting: Space Program
Subgenre: Survival Adventure
Time period setting: 2030s
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Science Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Scott Free Films, Kinberg Genre
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English
